captive.def: 俘虜、被監禁的人、受控制的、俘虜的人的人物、受控地 (俘)
captive.col: captive audience (被迫聽講的聽眾), held captive
captive.ex: The soldiers were held captive in a remote mountain camp.

captivity.ex: Wild animals often suffer when kept in long-term captivity.

captor.ex: He managed to escape from his captors during the night.

capture.def: 捕獲、佔領、奪取、拍攝、捕獲物、捕獲的人的人物、拍攝地 (捕)
capture.ex: She used her smartphone to capture the beautiful sunset.

captured.def: 被捕獲的、被佔領的人的人 (或物)
captured.ex: The captured village was reclaimed by the army yesterday.

car.def: 汽車、車廂、轎車的人物 (單數)
car.col: sports car, car park, cable car, car pooling
car.ex: He drives a small, fuel-efficient car to the office.

caracal.ex: The wild caracal is known for its distinctive black ear tufts.

carafe.ex: The waiter brought a glass carafe of cold sparkling water.

caramel.def: 焦糖、牛奶糖、褐色的、焦糖的人的人、褐色地 (焦糖)
caramel.ex: She drizzled sweet caramel sauce over the vanilla ice cream.

caramelize.ex: You need to caramelize the sugar slowly for the dessert.

carapace.ex: The turtle retreated into its hard, protective carapace.

carat.def: 克拉(寶石重量單位)、開(金純度單位) (單數)
carat.ex: She wore an expensive diamond ring of over three carats.

caravan.def: 商隊、旅行拖車、大篷車、結隊旅行、商隊的人的人物、旅行地 (隊)
caravan.ex: A long camel caravan traveled slowly across the vast desert.

caravanning.def: 乘大篷車旅行、商隊的人 (或物) (旅)
caravanning.ex: Caravanning is a popular way to explore the countryside.

caraway.def: 葛縷子、蘘荷的人 (或物) (藥)
caraway.ex: Caraway seeds are often added to rye bread for flavor.

carbide.ex: Calcium carbide is used to produce acetylene gas.

carbine.ex: The light carbine was easier for the soldiers to carry.

carbohydrate.ex: Pasta is a main source of complex carbohydrates for energy.

carbon.def: 碳、複寫紙、碳棒的人物 (單數)
carbon.col: carbon fiber, carbon footprint (碳足跡), carbon dioxide
carbon.ex: Carbon is a basic chemical element found in all life.

carbonaceous.ex: Scientists studied the carbonaceous remains in the ancient rock.

carbonate.def: 碳酸鹽、使碳酸飽和、碳酸的人的人物、飽和地 (碳酸)
carbonate.ex: Calcium carbonate is a common substance found in rocks.

carbonated.def: 充氣的、有碳酸的的人 (或物) (氣)
carbonated.ex: I prefer plain carbonated water to sugary sodas.

carboniferous.def: 含碳的、石炭紀的人的人 (或物)
carboniferous.ex: The park displays fossils from the Carboniferous era.

carbonize.def: 使碳化、使焦化的人的人、焦化地 (化)
carbonize.ex: The intense heat managed to carbonize the organic matter.

carborundum.ex: Carborundum is an extremely hard abrasive used for grinding.

carboy.ex: The acids were safely stored in a large glass carboy.

carbuncle.def: 癰、紅腫、紅寶石的人的人生的人 (單數)
carbuncle.ex: A painful carbuncle had formed on his neck during the week.

carburetor.ex: The mechanic had to clean the dirty car carburetor.

carcass.ex: A whale carcass had washed up on the local sandy beach.

carcinogen.ex: Tobacco smoke contains several well-known carcinogens.

carcinogenic.ex: Exposure to asbestos is known for its carcinogenic effect.

carcinoma.ex: The patient was successfully treated for skin carcinoma.

card.def: 卡片、紙牌、名片、梳理(羊毛)、在卡片上的人物 (單數)
card.col: credit card, playing card, birthday card, put one's cards on the table (坦白)
card.ex: He sent a funny birthday card to his younger sister.

cardboard.def: 硬紙板、厚紙板、脆弱的、沒份量的人物、紙板地 (紙板)
cardboard.col: cardboard box, cardboard character (不生動的人物)
cardboard.ex: Put all the waste paper into the large cardboard box.

cardiac.def: 心臟的人的人的人物、強心的的人物 (心)
cardiac.col: cardiac arrest (心臟驟停), cardiac surgery
cardiac.ex: The patient was rushed to the hospital with cardiac arrest.

cardigan.ex: She wore a cozy blue cardigan over her white summer dress.

cardinal.def: 主要的、基本的、樞機主教、紅衣、鮮紅的的人物 (核心)
cardinal.col: cardinal rule (核心規則), cardinal number (基數), cardinal sin (大錯)
cardinal.ex: Respect for the law is a cardinal rule of our society.

cardiogram.ex: The doctor studied the abnormal patterns on the cardiogram.

cardiograph.ex: The modern cardiograph provides a very accurate reading.
